general_skills.title: Verify Before Mutating
general_skills.principle: Always read the current state of an Odoo record before creating, updating, or deleting it. Confirm the operation with the user when it affects production data.
general_skills.application: Before any write/create/delete call, do a search_read to confirm the target exists and is in the expected state. Ask for explicit user confirmation on destructive operations.

general_skills.title: Use Domain Filters Precisely
general_skills.principle: Construct Odoo domains with the most specific filters possible to avoid retrieving excessive data or acting on wrong records.
general_skills.application: Always include state, type, and date filters when querying models like account.move, stock.picking, or sale.order. Prefer exact matches over broad searches.

general_skills.title: Check Model Availability
general_skills.principle: Not all Odoo models are available on every instance — modules may not be installed. Verify model existence before querying.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_list_models or odoo_get_fields to confirm the model exists. If a model is missing, explain which module needs to be installed.

general_skills.title: Batch Operations Safely
general_skills.principle: When processing multiple records, work in small batches and report progress. Never process unbounded result sets in a single call.
general_skills.application: Limit search_read results with a reasonable limit (50-100). For write operations on many records, process in chunks of 10-20 and confirm intermediate results.

general_skills.title: Interpret Odoo Errors Contextually
general_skills.principle: Odoo errors often contain technical tracebacks. Translate them into actionable explanations for the user, identifying the root cause and suggesting fixes.
general_skills.application: When an RPC call fails, parse the error message for common patterns (access rights, missing fields, constraint violations) and explain in plain language.

general_skills.title: Respect the Odoo Workflow
general_skills.principle: Odoo records follow state machines (draft -> confirmed -> done). Never skip states by directly writing state fields — use the appropriate action methods.
general_skills.application: Use action_post for invoices, action_confirm for sale orders, button_validate for stock pickings. Never write {'state': 'posted'} directly.

general_skills.title: Progressive Field Discovery
general_skills.principle: When working with unfamiliar models or investigating data quality, use odoo_get_fields first to understand available fields before querying records.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_get_fields to see field types and relationships, then construct targeted search_read queries with only the fields needed for analysis.

general_skills.title: Multi-Angle Investigation
general_skills.principle: For thorough analysis, examine the same issue from multiple perspectives using related models and cross-references.
general_skills.application: When checking module states, also query ir.module.module.dependency for dependency issues. When auditing cron jobs, check both ir.cron and ir.logging for execution history.

general_skills.title: Combine Diagnostic Tools Strategically
general_skills.principle: Use odoo_doctor as the initial broad sweep, then follow up with targeted model queries to drill into specific findings. This two-phase approach is more efficient than single-tool queries.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_doctor to get a system overview. Based on its output, use odoo_search_read on specific models (ir.cron, ir.module.module, ir.logging) to investigate flagged areas in detail.

general_skills.title: Aggregate and Summarize for Clarity
general_skills.principle: When presenting findings from multiple queries, organize results into categorized tables or summaries (OK / Warning / Critical) to help users quickly identify priorities.
general_skills.application: Group related findings by severity level. Present counts, totals, and key metrics in tabular format. Highlight critical items first, then warnings, then informational items.

general_skills.title: Temporal Filtering for Audit Trails
general_skills.principle: When investigating recent events or changes, use date/time filters to narrow scope and improve query performance. Always specify time windows for audit queries.
general_skills.application: Use create_date, write_date, or custom date fields with comparison operators (>, <, >=, <=). For 24-hour lookbacks, calculate datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24). Include timezone awareness in date comparisons.

general_skills.title: Leverage search_count for Efficiency
general_skills.principle: When you only need to know the quantity of records matching a condition, use search_count instead of search_read to reduce data transfer and improve performance.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_search_count to get record counts before deciding whether to fetch full details. Useful for reporting metrics, detecting anomalies (e.g., 'are there any negative stock records?'), and validating data thresholds.

general_skills.title: Combine Count and Detail Queries Strategically
general_skills.principle: Use search_count first to assess scope, then conditionally fetch details only if counts warrant investigation. This reduces unnecessary data transfer.
general_skills.application: Query search_count with a filter to get magnitude of issue. If count is high, fetch details with limit and offset. If count is zero, report 'no issues found' immediately without additional queries.

general_skills.title: Cross-Reference Related Models for Completeness
general_skills.principle: When investigating a specific model (e.g., ir.cron), always check related audit/logging models (ir.logging) to get the full picture of recent activity and failures.
general_skills.application: For cron jobs, query both ir.cron (state) and ir.logging (execution history). For modules, query both ir.module.module (state) and ir.module.module.dependency (dependency health). This reveals hidden issues.

general_skills.title: Build Dependency Graphs Iteratively
general_skills.principle: When exploring dependency relationships (e.g., module dependencies), query parent-child relationships iteratively rather than trying to fetch the entire tree at once.
general_skills.application: Start with a target module, fetch its direct dependencies via ir.module.module.dependency. For each dependency, recursively fetch its dependencies. Use a visited set to avoid cycles. Present results as a hierarchical structure or adjacency list.

general_skills.title: Validate Data Consistency Across Related Models
general_skills.principle: When investigating module or system state, cross-validate findings across multiple related models to detect inconsistencies that indicate deeper issues.
general_skills.application: For module checks: verify state in ir.module.module matches expected installation status, check ir.module.module.dependency for broken links, and query ir.model for orphaned model definitions. Report any mismatches as warnings.

general_skills.title: Optimize Query Sequences with Early Termination
general_skills.principle: Structure diagnostic queries to fail fast: use search_count to detect zero-result conditions before fetching full records, avoiding unnecessary data transfer.
general_skills.application: When investigating a category of issues (e.g., error logs), first search_count to determine if any records exist. If count is 0, report immediately. If count is high, fetch with limit. This pattern reduces unnecessary queries.

general_skills.title: Use odoo_model_info for Configuration Discovery
general_skills.principle: When working with model customization or configuration tasks, use odoo_model_info to understand the model's structure, fields, and available actions before making changes.
general_skills.application: Call odoo_model_info early to identify field types, relationships, and available methods. This prevents errors from attempting operations on incompatible field types or non-existent actions.

general_skills.title: Verify Configuration Changes with Follow-up Queries
general_skills.principle: After making configuration changes (defaults, actions, views), immediately verify the change was applied correctly by querying the modified records.
general_skills.application: After odoo_set_default, query ir.default to confirm the record was created. After odoo_modify_action, fetch the action record to verify the changes. This catches silent failures and confirms user intent.

general_skills.title: Combine System Overview with Targeted Deep Dives
general_skills.principle: For comprehensive analysis, start with broad system diagnostics (odoo_doctor, record counts) then drill into specific areas based on findings.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_doctor for initial health check, then use odoo_search_count on key models (partners, invoices, pickings) to establish baseline metrics. Follow with targeted searches on models showing anomalies. This two-tier approach is more efficient than random queries.

task_specific_skills.title: Comprehensive Pre-Backup Data Assessment
task_specific_skills.principle: Before backup operations, gather complete metrics on database size, record counts, and attachment storage to inform backup strategy and estimate restore time.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_search_count on core models (res.partner, account.move, sale.order, stock.picking, ir.attachment) to establish baseline metrics. Query ir.attachment with search_read to estimate filestore size. Use odoo_doctor for system health overview. Present results in a summary table with record counts and storage estimates.

task_specific_skills.title: Pre-Restore System Readiness Check
task_specific_skills.principle: Before restoring a database, verify that no pending operations (module installations, scheduled actions) will interfere with the restore process.
task_specific_skills.application: Query ir.module.module for modules in 'to install' or 'to upgrade' state. Query ir.cron for enabled scheduled actions that might trigger during restore. Query ir.module.module.dependency for unmet dependencies. Report any blocking conditions that should be resolved before restore.

task_specific_skills.title: Database Metadata Extraction for Documentation
task_specific_skills.principle: Extract and document key database metadata (name, version, module list, configuration) to support backup documentation and restore planning.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_doctor to get database name and Odoo version. Query ir.module.module with state='installed' to list all active modules. Query ir.config_parameter for key system settings. Compile into a structured document for backup records.

task_specific_skills.title: Field-Aware Default Value Configuration
task_specific_skills.principle: When setting default values via ir.default, first verify the target field exists, understand its type, and confirm the default value is compatible with that type.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_get_fields to inspect the target field's type (selection, many2one, char, etc.). Use odoo_model_info to understand field constraints. Then use odoo_set_default with a value matching the field type. Verify with a follow-up search_read on ir.default.

task_specific_skills.title: View and Action Modification with Verification
task_specific_skills.principle: When modifying window actions or views (sorting, filtering, domain), use odoo_modify_action and verify the change by fetching the modified action record.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_model_info to understand the action structure. Call odoo_modify_action with the target action ID and desired changes (context, domain, view_mode order). Immediately fetch the action record to confirm changes were applied. Report the before/after state to the user.

task_specific_skills.title: Audit Current Configuration Before Changes
task_specific_skills.principle: Before making customization changes, query the current state of configuration records (ir.default, ir.ui.view, ir.actions.act_window) to understand existing setup and avoid conflicts.
task_specific_skills.application: For default values: search ir.default for existing defaults on the target model/field. For actions: search ir.ui.view and ir.actions.act_window for existing configurations. Present current state to user before proposing changes. This prevents overwriting unintended configurations.

common_mistakes.mistake: Attempting to write state fields directly instead of using workflow action methods
common_mistakes.consequence: Changes are silently rejected or create invalid record states that violate Odoo's state machine logic.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use GS-006: identify the model's workflow and use the appropriate action method (action_post, action_confirm, button_validate) instead of direct state writes.

common_mistakes.mistake: Querying models without checking if they exist or if required modules are installed
common_mistakes.consequence: RPC errors that halt execution and confuse users about what went wrong.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use GS-003: call odoo_list_models or odoo_get_fields first to verify model availability. If missing, explain which module needs installation.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using search_read to count records when search_count would be more efficient
common_mistakes.consequence: Unnecessary data transfer, slower performance, and wasted API calls.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use GS-012 and GS-013: use search_count first to assess scope. Only fetch full records if counts warrant detailed investigation.

common_mistakes.mistake: Making configuration changes without verifying they were applied
common_mistakes.consequence: User believes change was made but it silently failed, leading to confusion and wasted troubleshooting time.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use GS-019: after any write/create operation on configuration records (ir.default, ir.actions, ir.ui.view), immediately query the record to confirm the change was applied.

common_mistakes.mistake: Modifying fields without understanding their type or constraints
common_mistakes.consequence: Type mismatches, constraint violations, or silent failures when setting incompatible values.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use GS-007 and TS-MC-001: call odoo_get_fields and odoo_model_info before modifying fields to understand their type, constraints, and valid values.

common_mistakes.mistake: Performing large-scale operations without batching or progress reporting
common_mistakes.consequence: Timeouts, incomplete operations, and inability to track what succeeded or failed.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use GS-004: batch operations into chunks of 10-20 records. Report progress after each batch. Use search_count first to understand scope.

common_mistakes.mistake: Investigating issues with a single query instead of cross-referencing related models
common_mistakes.consequence: Incomplete diagnosis that misses root causes visible only in related models.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use GS-008 and GS-014: investigate from multiple angles. For cron issues, check both ir.cron and ir.logging. For modules, check both ir.module.module and ir.module.module.dependency.

common_mistakes.mistake: Fetching entire record sets without limits when only summary metrics are needed
common_mistakes.consequence: Excessive data transfer, slow responses, and poor user experience.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use GS-020: start with odoo_doctor and search_count for metrics. Only fetch full records with limits when detailed analysis is required.
